DiagMessage.MessageText: FW Problem: type 0 - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
FW Problem: type 1 - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Stack Overflow Error - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Move Generator Problem - The move generator failed to initiate the move. Adjust your constraints and contact Teknic if the problem persists
ESC EEPROM version mismatch - Load a compatible version of the ESC EEPROM file for this firmware
Internal Power Supply Problem - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Non-Volatile memory dead - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Flash has been corrupted - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Firmware ROMsum error - Load config file compatible with motor's firmware version or reset motor to factory defaults
ADC values saturated - Phase current beyond allowed ADC limit. Probable cause: incorrect tuning or wrong config file
E-Stop - ESC key or E-Stop button was pressed by the user in ClearView
Old config file version - Probable cause: Firmware updated after config file was loaded. Reset to factory defaults or load a new config file
CPU 2 failed to communicate; fully power-cycle the motor and contact Teknic if the problem persists
Move canceled (Range) - Move target out of range. Confirm the move target is within defined software position limits
RAS change rejected - Unexpected error
Speed too high for RAS - Unexpected error
Current sensor HW fault - Unit requires repair. Return to Teknic
Move canceled (Limit Switch) - Limit switch activated
Move canceled (Soft Limit) - Target position violates software position limit settings
D-Current Heater Error - Possible causes: too much shaft motion
Vector Refine Error - Contact Teknic if the problem persists
Encoder noise - Fully remove logic power and restart the motor. Contact Teknic if the problem persists
Overspeed timeout exceeded - Motor has exceeded the motor's Max Speed
Motor phase overload - Phase current beyond allowed ADC limit. Probable cause: Incorrect tuning or wrong config file
Motor parameter error - Unit requires corrected factory settings. Contact Teknic
Hard Stop gave way - Mechanical Hard Stop was detected during homing but it gave way before homing was completed
Following error limit exceeded - Possible causes: incorrect tuning
RMS torque limit exceeded - Possible causes: incorrect tuning
Step input timing error - Possible causes: Step input pulse width too short
MagAlign distance error - Distance traveled does not match expected value. Possible cause: motor against an end stop
MagAlign direction error - Direction traveled is incorrect. Probable cause: external forces during MagAlign procedure
Sensorless startup error - Error during sensorless startup. Probable causes: external force or bus voltage too low during sensorless startup
Excessive motor temp - Possible causes: Ambient temperature too high for motor load
Excessive bus current - Probable causes: Incorrect tuning or wrong config file
Max bus voltage exceeded - Probable cause: Large regenerated voltage upon deceleration. Lower velocity/acceleration or enable the Vector Regen Shunt (VRS) under the 'Details' menu in ClearView
Bus voltage was lost or was too low
Excessive bus current - Probable causes: Incorrect tuning or wrong config file
Power Mismatched - Probable causes: AC power problem
Encoder Index Problem - Fully remove logic power and restart the motor. Contact Teknic if the problem persists
Bus under Operating Voltage - Possible causes: Brown out
Controller Disconnected - ClearView or EtherCAT master has disconnected unexpectedly and the abort connection option code triggered a Fault Reaction
Interlock Triggered - The input used as an Interlock has been triggered. The motor will stay disabled until the interlock input returns to its normal state
Encoder Index Problem - Fully remove logic power and restart the motor. Contact Teknic if the problem persists
Invalid EtherCAT Cycle Time - Must be a multiple of 62.5us
SDO write failed - Probable cause: ClearView has Full Access of the drive
Transition 'Pre-Op' to 'Safe-Op' failed - Sync Manager length error
Transition 'Pre-Op' to 'Safe-Op' failed - Sync Manager address error
Transition 'Pre-Op' to 'Safe-Op' failed - Sync Manager settings error
Transition 'Pre-Op' to 'Safe-Op' failed - Sync Manager address ranges overlap
PDO mapping error - Unable to map object
PDO mapping error - Double mapping of object
Entered OP mode while the drive is in fault
Errors have been cleared
